<br><div class="config-table-header">Syft Configuration</div>

<table class="config-table syft-config-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-config-key"><abbr class="header-help" title="The configuration field name used in Syft/Grype application configuration">Configuration Key</abbr></th>
      <th class="col-description"><abbr class="header-help" title="Explanation of what the configuration option controls">Description</abbr></th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-config-key"><code>dotnet.dep-packages-must-claim-dll</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Allows for deps.json packages to be included only if there is a runtime/resource DLL claimed in the deps.json targets section. This does not require such claimed DLLs to exist on disk. The behavior of this</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-config-key"><code>dotnet.dep-packages-must-have-dll</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Allows for deps.json packages to be included only if there is a DLL on disk for that package.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-config-key"><code>dotnet.propagate-dll-claims-to-parents</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Allows for deps.json packages to be included if any child (transitive) package claims a DLL. This applies to both the claims configuration and evidence-on-disk configurations.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-config-key"><code>dotnet.relax-dll-claims-when-bundling-detected</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Will look for indications of IL bundle tooling via deps.json package names and, if found (and this config option is enabled), will relax the DepPackagesMustClaimDLL value to <code>false</code> only in those cases.</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
